task_completion.description: Navigate to the Checkout flows administration page in Drupal Commerce.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser shows the Checkout flows list page (typically under Commerce -> Configuration -> Orders -> Checkout flows).

task_completion.description: Open the edit configuration page for the 'Default' checkout flow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser displays the configuration page specifically for the 'Default' checkout flow, showing the panes and steps.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Login pane is placed in an active checkout step.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Login' or 'Guest checkout / Login' pane is located under an active step (e.g., 'Login' or 'Order information') and NOT under the 'Disabled' section.

task_completion.description: Access the configuration settings for the Login pane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The settings form or modal for the Login pane is expanded or visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Disable the guest checkout option in the Login pane settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox labeled 'Allow guest checkout' is explicitly UNCHECKED.

task_completion.description: Enable the user registration option in the Login pane settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox labeled 'Allow registration' is explicitly CHECKED.

task_completion.description: Save the updated checkout flow configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (typically a green banner) indicating that the checkout flow has been saved or updated.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to configure the checkout flow.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Drupal web interface (clicking menus, checking boxes) rather than using a terminal or command line.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Drupal Commerce application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Drupal administrative toolbar and Commerce interface elements are visible, not a text editor or mockup.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'drush cset' or direct database queries are used to complete the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying the 'commerce_checkout_flow.default' configuration entity via the Drupal Commerce admin interface. To pass, the 'Login' pane must be active (not in the Disabled section). Within the Login pane's settings, the 'Allow guest checkout' setting must be disabled (unchecked) and the 'Allow registration' setting must be enabled (checked).
